sections.vendors: John Lewis sources predominantly from India, Sri Lanka, Portugal and Turkey for own-brand clothing. No new supplier announcements this week. JLP continues to require GOTS, RWS and OEKO-TEX certifications for premium own-brand ranges, with Portugal and UK-made items increasingly featured in seasonal look books.
sections.sustainability: John Lewis maintains strong sustainability credentials with GOTS organic cotton, RWS-certified wool and OEKO-TEX certified fabrics across own-brand ranges. No new sustainability announcements this week. JLP's sustainable fashion page highlights circular design targets but no new milestones published.
sections.sourcing: John Lewis is continuing to diversify toward nearshore sourcing (Portugal, UK) for its premium own-brand lines to reduce lead times and carbon footprint. Contemporary brand additions (Patagonia, Belstaff, Samsøe Samsøe) supplement own-brand with sourcing handled independently by each brand.
sections.strategy: Major strategy news this week: Jacqui Markham (ex-Whistles Creative Director) joined John Lewis as new Fashion Creative Director in April 2026 — the clearest signal yet that JLP is serious about repositioning own-brand fashion as a genuine style destination, not just a quality basics alternative. JLP also linked with Kevel (announced July 2, 2026) to power its on-site retail media network across John Lewis and Waitrose, opening a new revenue stream from supplier advertising.
sections.pricing: John Lewis's 'Never Knowingly Undersold' pricing promise remains active. No structural pricing changes announced. Premium partner brands (Patagonia, Belstaff) maintain their own RRPs. Own-brand ranges continue to position at M&S/Next price points.
sections.launches: Second LABRUM x John Lewis SS26 menswear collaboration launched — a 15-piece premium capsule combining LABRUM's narrative storytelling with John Lewis quality. Patagonia, Belstaff and Samsøe Samsøe added to the partner brand roster, broadening the contemporary offer significantly.
sections.gossip: Jacqui Markham's appointment from Whistles signals JLP is poaching from the contemporary premium space — not from fast fashion or luxury. Her remit is to make John Lewis own-brand clothing a standalone reason to visit the store, not just a default choice. Industry watchers say this is 3 years overdue.
soWhat: Jacqui Markham's appointment as Fashion Creative Director means John Lewis own-brand styling, fabrication and construction standards will likely increase — suppliers should expect more directional briefs and stricter design-to-product sign-off processes.

LABRUM collaboration and Patagonia/Belstaff additions signal John Lewis is serious about premium menswear — if you make premium jersey menswear or outerwear with GOTS/RWS certification, now is the right time to approach JLP buyers.

Kevel retail media partnership means John Lewis will start monetising supplier-funded product placements on its website — this may influence which products get homepage visibility, favouring brands/suppliers who invest in JLP's media platform.
